description: Self-defined eco-extremist group that emerged in Mexico in 2011. It advocates for the destruction of technological civilization and has claimed responsibility for letter bombs and other attacks, though many claims have been disputed. The group draws inspiration from Ted Kaczynski and has attempted to extend its influence internationally, though its presence outside Mexico remains unconfirmed.

country_links.evidence_quote: the 'Wild Constellations' cell claimed to leave explosives in the Argentine Nanotechnology Foundation, threats by e-mail to several researchers and a package bomb at the Retiro bus station and the National Technological University
country_links.context: ITS's 'Wild Constellations' cell claimed to leave explosives at the Argentine Nanotechnology Foundation and other locations in Argentina.

country_links.evidence_quote: various attacks in 2013, 2014 and 2016 against street furniture and vehicles of the security forces in Porto Alegre (Brazil) were claimed by ITS from the beginning, as the beginning of an international armed struggle campaign.
country_links.context: ITS claimed attacks in Porto Alegre, Brazil, as part of an international armed struggle campaign.

country_links.evidence_quote: The first ITS attack in Chile took place on February 18, 2016, The group "Sureños Incivilizados" made its appearance and totally burned a Transantiago bus in broad daylight...
country_links.context: ITS claimed responsibility for multiple attacks in Chile, including arson of a bus, an incendiary device at a university, and explosive packages.

country_links.evidence_quote: At the end of the same year, at Christmas 2018, a device exploded at the entrance of the Agios Dionysios Orthodox Church in Athens . The "Iconoclasta Sect" claimed responsibility for the attack...
country_links.context: ITS claimed responsibility for a device explosion at a church in Athens, and Greek nationals were arrested in connection with the Edinburgh bomb.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Scottish police carried out a controlled explosion of an explosive device near Edinburgh Castle on January 11, 2018. ITS claimed responsibility for the placement of the explosive device through blog posts and e-mails to local media.
country_links.context: ITS claimed responsibility for an explosive device near Edinburgh Castle, which was later deactivated by Scottish police.
